system.coreNature:
You read a sad story. You cry. Do you think that's sinister, as if someone has artificially taken over your emotions? Of course not. That's just what art is.

Anyone who's ever met a Dictator would disagree.

By performing, they alter other people's emotional states. Dictators play people like a musician plays a harp. They can pluck the strings. They can snap them.

They're like bards, if everyone was fucking petrified of bards.

PLAY A DICTATOR IF YOU WANT TO...

  • Be an artist whose performances warp others' emotions, and even create magical effects.
  • Use your imagination and creativity to work out exactly what your awful gift can do.
  • Be hated and feared for very good reasons.

coreAbility.name: A LITTLE EMOTIONAL NUDGE
coreAbility.description: When you have your D4 and could get an edge from subtle emotional manipulation, roll it as part of your dice pool and increase any other dice's result by its value.

looks.name: Flamboyant, straight out of a stage show

fields.label: Emotion Palette
fields.description:
SELECT YOUR EMOTION PALETTE

Choose a set of emotions to be your focus: these are the emotions you manipulate.

fields.label: The Tell
fields.description: The Voice lets you issue commands with magical force. When you control emotions, you merely speak commands aloud, but something gives you away. What is it?
choices: Strange smells emerge from your mouth (scent lingers)

fields.label: The Performance
fields.initial:
"

To use The Voice, pick the emotion(s) and roll a Cha dice pool plus your D4. Lose 1 success for each target past the first. The D4 shows the effect’s Intensity, and each successes lets you add or subtract 1 from it. The final Intensity affects the Audience according to your goal – Control or Attack.

Control: Manipulate them with the emotion and steal their agency. +1 Disadvantage per Intensity on actions opposing the compulsion, +1 Advantage on actions that support it. Complete control if Intensity exceeds Willpower.

Attack: Make them feel so hard it hurts. They take +1 Disadvantage per Intensity on attacks, and are killed or incapacitated if Intensity exceeds Willpower.

Pass your D4 to a player whose character you’re influencing. To affect anyone else with your powers, you must reclaim your D4. This is instant and ends that compulsion.

INTENSITY OF EFFECT
  1. Enough to influence them.
  2. They feel it intensely.
  3. They feel it as intensely as most people will ever feel it.
  4. More intense than love for a soulmate.
  5. All-consuming obsession.
  6. Supernaturally intense. Likely lethal.

If Intensity < Willpower, targets may resist with a Wis roll at a Difficulty of 1/2 Intensity.

On a Crit Fail, you’ve broken them; the influence is permanent.

"

fields.label: It's Not What You Know
fields: {"key":"choice","type":"select","label":"Benefit Choice","choices":["A Working Artist (Pick Once)","Henchperson (Pick Once)","Improve Henchperson","Agent"],"initial":"","hint":"Choose one benefit from this advancement"}

{"key":"henchpersonBinding","type":"select","label":"Henchperson - How Are They Bound?","choices":["A critical fail emotion","A promise","A need that you fulfill","They resent their condition","Their friends resent their condition"],"initial":"","hint":"If you chose Henchperson: how are they bound to you?"}

fields.label: One Step Ahead
fields.initial:
"

One Step Ahead: You may ask the GM for a hint about an NPC's emotional state or motivations. The GM must answer truthfully.

This represents your keen understanding of social dynamics and emotional manipulation.

"

fields.label: The Gig
fields.initial:
"

The Gig: You have a regular performance venue or gig that provides you with income and social connections.

Work with your GM to define the venue, audience, and what you perform.

"

fields.label: Fanbase
fields.initial:
"

Fanbase: You have a dedicated following of fans who adore your performances.

Once per session, you can call upon your fans for assistance, information, or cover. The GM determines what help they can reasonably provide.

"

fields.label: The Gang
fields.initial:
"

The Gang: You have a crew of loyal followers who help you with your schemes.

The gang can perform simple tasks, provide backup, or cause distractions. Work with your GM to define the gang's capabilities and limitations.

"

fields.label: Emotional Capital
fields.initial:
"

Emotional Capital: You've built up significant social influence and emotional connections.

Once per session, you can leverage your emotional capital to gain a significant favor, piece of information, or advantage in a social situation.

"

fields.label: Carving Out Their Heart
fields.initial:
"

Carving Out Their Heart: You can use The Voice to permanently alter someone's emotional state or personality.

This is a powerful and dangerous ability. Work with your GM to determine the exact effects and consequences.

"

fields.label: Portrait of the Artist as a Monster
fields: {"key":"description","type":"html","label":"Monster Form Description","initial":"","hint":"Describe your monstrous form when you fully embrace your power"}

{"key":"traits","type":"multiSelect","label":"Monster Traits (Pick 4)","choices":["Invulnerable to one common form of harm","Attacks cause 2 Wounds (Special)","Invulnerable to emotional control","Can attack at range","Health is 2x CON","Can use The Voice silently with a glance","Can fly","Can pass through objects","Can disguise its monstrous nature"],"initial":[],"hint":"Select exactly 4 traits for your monster form"}

fields.label: The Great Dictator
fields.initial:
"

The Great Dictator: You have achieved the pinnacle of your power as a Dictator.

You can now affect entire crowds or populations with your performances. The scope and scale of your emotional manipulation is limited only by your ambition and the GM's discretion.

However, your Tell becomes more pronounced, and the supernatural nature of your power is increasingly difficult to hide.

"

fields.label: Live at The Witch Trials (Level 4 Automatic)
choices: The Hunters have chosen to ignore you

fields.label: Live at The Witch Trials - Details
